title: PhD: Electrical property mapping of the human body using magnetic resonance imaging (MRI)
description:
Funding: **Fully funded doctoral contract**
Start: Ideally October 2026, open for negotiation
Topic: Developing new MRI methods to map electrical properties of human tissues (tumor detection & treatment planning) (see attachment for details: [OffreThèse_IADI_IRM_propriétés_électriques_2026.docx](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27217940/OffreThese_IADI_IRM_proprietes_electriques_2026.docx))
MRI lab with access to clinical 1.5T & research 3T MRI scanners
Supervisor: Freddy Odille (Inserm Research Director)
Profile: Master's or engineering degree (biomedical eng., physics, maths, or data science) · Matlab/Python · English proficiency
French: **Not required**

Apply: CV + cover letter → freddy.odille@inserm.fr

title: PhD Position: Investigating metabolites present in the spinal cord for the study of multiple sclerosis using 7T CEST MRI
institution: Centre de Résonance Magnétique Biologique et Médicale (CRMBM-CEMEREM ; amU, CNRS & APHM), La Timone Hospital, Marseille, France
description:
We are happy to share an opportunity to join [CRMBM](https://crmbm.univ-amu.fr/) in Marseille to work on 7T MRI of the spinal cord, chemical exchange saturation transfer (CEST), parallel transmit (pTx), and multiple sclerosis! We are recruiting a PhD candidate for a fully funded position starting in January 2027. You will work within a highly interdisciplinary environment, collaborating closely with teams at CRMBM, and more specifically with Aurélien Destruel. Great opportunity to work on a state-of-the-art technique with great potential for clinical applications !
Host laboratory: Center for Magnetic Resonance in Biology and Medicine, Marseille, France
